name: Cascade d'Ars and Guzet Pond Loop
name_original: Cascade d'Ars – boucle au départ de d'Aulus-les-Bains– Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
description: Hike the difficult 8.0-mile Cascade d'Ars and Guzet Pond Loop for stunning waterfalls, tranquil lakes, and Pyrenean views.
region_intro: Hiking around Aulus-Les-Bains offers access to the Ariège Pyrenees, characterized by its mountainous terrain and dense forests. The region is situated in the Garbet river valley, providing a natural setting for outdoor activities. Notable features include the Cascade d'Ars waterfall, the Etang du Garbet, and the surrounding peaks. The area's network of trails caters to various fitness levels, from accessible paths to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Aulus-Les-Bains The most popular hiking route is Cascade d'Ars – loop starting from d'Aulus-les-Bains– Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes about 5 hours 35 minutes to complete, leading to the multi-tiered Cascade d'Ars waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Etang du Garbet – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This route offers views of the picturesque Etang du Garbet lake. Local hikers also love the La Pique Rouge de Bassiès – hike starting from Coumebière – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, an 11.3 miles (18.1 km) trail leading through the Pyrenees mountains, often completed in about 8 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Aulus-Les-Bains is defined by Pyrenees mountain panoramas, dense forests, and river valleys. The network includes 2 easy, 12 moderate, and 15 difficult routes, offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Aulus-Les-Bains are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Aulus-Les-Bains's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: There are over 29 hiking routes in the Aulus-Les-Bains area. This network includes 2 easy, 12 moderate, and 15 difficult routes, offering options for various ability levels within the Pyrenees mountains.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: Yes, Aulus-Les-Bains offers a few easy trails suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the region's diverse network includes paths designed for less strenuous walks, often found along the Garbet river valley.

Q: Can I hike to the Cascade d'Ars waterfall?
A: Absolutely. The magnificent Cascade d'Ars is a major attraction. You can reach it via several trails, including a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3993680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ars Waterfall (Middle) – View of the Cascade d'Ars loop from Aulus-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. The waterfall has three successive levels and is considered one of the most beautiful in the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Aulus-Les-Bains area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949302072/cascade-d-ars-loop-starting-from-d-aulus-les-bains-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascade d'Ars – loop starting from d'Aulus-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 12.9 km loop that takes approximately 5 hours 35 minutes.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: Hiking around Aulus-Les-Bains offers diverse natural features. You'll encounter stunning Pyrenees mountain panoramas, dense forests, and charming river valleys like the Garbet. Notable spots include the multi-tiered Cascade d'Ars waterfall and the picturesque Etang du Garbet lake, where swimming might be possible on warm days.

Q: Are there any lakes or ponds accessible by hiking trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956907830/etang-du-garbet-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etang du Garbet&lt;/a&gt; is a popular hiking destination. This moderate 7.6 km path leads to a picturesque small lake, offering a beautiful spot to relax, and on warm days, swimming might be an option.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 430 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning Pyrenees mountain panoramas, the tranquility of the dense forests, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, Aulus-Les-Bains is a gateway to the Pyrenees and offers 15 difficult routes for experienced hikers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956914363/la-pique-rouge-de-bassies-hike-starting-from-coumebiere-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pique Rouge de Bassiès – hike starting from Coumebière&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.1 km trail that typically takes over 8 hours to complete.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several notable landmarks. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agnès Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lers Pass&lt;/a&gt; are popular. For those seeking higher altitudes, peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/480323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pica d'Estats&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcalm Peak&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach.

Q: Is Aulus-Les-Bains part of any long-distance hiking trails?
A: Yes, Aulus-Les-Bains is directly situated on the GR10, also known as the Pyrenean Way. This renowned long-distance trail traverses the Pyrenees from the Atlantic to the Mediterranean, offering exceptional experiences for avid hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: The best time for hiking in Aulus-Les-Bains is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October) when the weather is milder and trails are clear of snow. Summer offers warm days, while spring and autumn provide vibrant foliage and fewer crowds. Higher altitude routes may still have snow earlier or later in the season.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming after a hike?
A: While not explicitly designated for wild swimming, the picturesque Etang du Garbet lake is a popular spot where swimming might be possible on warm days. The Garbet river also flows through the village, offering refreshing spots.
tips: Great hike to do with good intermediate level mountain shoes. The view is beautiful !

Located near a water point, this small rock is an ideal spot for pitching your tent or having a picnic!

A small place to make a fire is also present, be careful all the same especially in times of drought 😉
Located in the Couserans massif, at an altitude of 1,458 meters, the Guzet pond is bordered by vegetation, which makes the place all the more idyllic.

faqs.question: How long does the Cascade d'Ars and Guzet Pond Loop typically take to hike?
faqs.answer: This challenging loop usually takes around 5.5 to 6.5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the views. It covers approximately 12.9 kilometers with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the trail and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Aulus-les-Bains. While specific parking details aren't provided, Aulus-les-Bains is the common access point for the Cascade d'Ars, and you would generally find parking available in or near the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this loop?
faqs.answer: The full loop incorporating Étang de Guzet is rated as difficult. This is due to the substantial elevation gain (over 800 meters) and descent, as well as potentially demanding sections with rocky paths and areas that can be wet or swampy, especially after rain. It's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter gradual climbs on wide gravel roads, lush beech and fir forests, and steeper sections with numerous switchbacks. Expect rocky paths, passages through peat and streams, and even avalanche corridors. Some parts can be wet and muddy, so appropriate footwear is essential.

faqs.question: What are the main natural attractions along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the magnificent View of the Cascade d'Ars, one of the most beautiful waterfalls in the French Pyrenees, plunging over 200 meters. You'll also enjoy tranquil views of the mirror-like Guzet Pond, and expansive vistas from viewpoints like Roc de Pich.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is after the snow has completely melted, typically from mid-June through early autumn. May and June offer the most impressive flow for the Cascade d'Ars due to snowmelt, but some sections, particularly avalanche corridors, might still be dangerous until later in June.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a challenging hike. Be prepared for significant elevation changes, potentially rocky and slippery terrain, and sections that can be wet. Trail markings might not always be very visible, so navigation skills are important. Always check weather conditions before you go, and be aware that avalanche corridors can be dangerous outside of summer months.

faqs.question: Is the Cascade d'Ars and Guzet Pond Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, as it's located within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises. However, visitors are expected to respect the park's regulations, which include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and leaving no trace.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other well-known paths. You will find yourself on parts of the long-distance hiking trail 'La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.3' for about 65% of the route, and also briefly on the 'Paserelle d'Ars' trail.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While Aulus-les-Bains is the starting point, direct public transport options to the specific trailhead can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the village, as it offers more flexibility for accessing the start of the loop.
